 17→package com.ichi2.anki.analytics
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 21→import android.os.Build
 22→import android.webkit.WebSettings
 23→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 24→import androidx.core.content.edit
 25→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.GoogleAnalytics
 26→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.GoogleAnalyticsConfig
 27→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.httpclient.OkHttpClientImpl
 28→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.request.DefaultRequest
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.BuildConfig
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 33→import com.ichi2.utils.DisplayUtils
 34→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup
 35→import com.ichi2.utils.WebViewDebugging.hasSetDataDirectory
 36→import org.acra.ACRA
 37→import org.acra.util.Installation
 38→import timber.log.Timber
 39→
 40→@KotlinCleanup("see if we can make variables lazy, or properties without the `s` prefix")
 41→object UsageAnalytics {
 42→    const val ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY = "analyticsOptIn"
 43→
 44→    @KotlinCleanup("lateinit")
 45→    private var sAnalytics: GoogleAnalytics? = null
 46→    private var sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler: Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler? = null
 47→    private var sOptIn = false
 48→    private var sAnalyticsTrackingId: String? = null
 49→    private var sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = -1
 50→
 51→    /**
 52→     * Initialize the analytics provider - must be called prior to sending anything.
 53→     * Usage after that is static
 54→     * Note: may need to implement sampling strategy internally to limit hits, or not track Reviewer...
 55→     *
 56→     * @param context required to look up the analytics codes for the app
 57→     */
 58→    @Synchronized
 59→    fun initialize(context: Context): GoogleAnalytics? {
 60→        Timber.i("initialize()")
 61→        if (sAnalytics == null) {
 62→            Timber.d("App tracking id 'tid' = %s", getAnalyticsTag(context))
 63→            val gaConfig = GoogleAnalyticsConfig()
 64→                .setBatchingEnabled(true)
 65→                .setSamplePercentage(getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context))
 66→                .setBatchSize(1) // until this handles application termination we will lose hits if batch>1
 67→            sAnalytics = GoogleAnalytics.builder()
 68→                .withTrackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 69→                .withConfig(gaConfig)
 70→                .withDefaultRequest(
 71→                    AndroidDefaultRequest()
 72→                        .setAndroidRequestParameters(context)
 73→                        .applicationName(context.getString(R.string.app_name))
 74→                        .applicationVersion(BuildConfig.VERSION_CODE.toString())
 75→                        .applicationId(BuildConfig.APPLICATION_ID)
 76→                        .trackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 77→                        .clientId(Installation.id(context))
 78→                        .anonymizeIp(context.resources.getBoolean(R.bool.ga_anonymizeIp))
 79→                )
 80→                .withHttpClient(OkHttpClientImpl(gaConfig))
 81→                .build()
 82→        }
 83→        installDefaultExceptionHandler()
 84→        val userPrefs = context.sharedPrefs()
 85→        optIn = userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
 86→        userPrefs.registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener { sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String? ->
 87→            if (key == ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY) {
 88→                val newValue = sharedPreferences.getBoolean(key, false)
 89→                Timber.i("Setting analytics opt-in to: %b", newValue)
 90→                optIn = newValue
 91→            }
 92→        }
 93→        return sAnalytics
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    private fun getAnalyticsTag(context: Context): String? {
 97→        if (sAnalyticsTrackingId == null) {
 98→            sAnalyticsTrackingId = context.getString(R.string.ga_trackingId)
 99→        }
100→        return sAnalyticsTrackingId
101→    }
102→
103→    private fun getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context: Context): Int {
104→        if (sAnalyticsSamplePercentage == -1) {
105→            sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = context.resources.getInteger(R.integer.ga_sampleFrequency)
106→        }
107→        return sAnalyticsSamplePercentage
108→    }
109→
110→    fun setDevMode() {
111→        Timber.d("setDevMode() re-configuring for development analytics tagging")
112→        sAnalyticsTrackingId = "UA-125800786-2"
113→        sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = 100
114→        reInitialize()
115→    }
116→
117→    /**
118→     * We want to send an analytics hit on any exception, then chain to other handlers (e.g., ACRA)
119→     */
120→    @Synchronized
121→    private fun installDefaultExceptionHandler() {
122→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = Thread.getDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler()
123→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler { thread: Thread?, throwable: Throwable ->
124→            sendAnalyticsException(throwable, true)
125→            if (thread == null) {
126→                Timber.w("unexpected: thread was null")
127→                return@setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler
128→            }
129→            sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler!!.uncaughtException(thread, throwable)
130→        }
131→    }
132→
133→    /**
134→     * Reset the default exception handler
135→     */
136→    @Synchronized
137→    private fun unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler() {
138→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler)
139→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = null
140→    }
141→
142→    /**
143→     * Allow users to enable or disable analytics
144→     */
145→    @set:Synchronized
146→    private var optIn: Boolean
147→        get() {
148→            Timber.d("getOptIn() status: %s", sOptIn)
149→            return sOptIn
150→        }
151→        private set(optIn) {
152→            Timber.i("setOptIn(): from %s to %s", sOptIn, optIn)
153→            sOptIn = optIn
154→            sAnalytics!!.flush()
155→            sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled = optIn
156→            sAnalytics!!.performSamplingElection()
157→            Timber.d("setOptIn() optIn / sAnalytics.config().enabled(): %s/%s", sOptIn, sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled)
158→        }
159→
160→    /**
161→     * Set the analytics up to log things, goes to hit validator. Experimental.
162→     *
163→     * @param dryRun set to true if you want to log analytics hit but not dispatch
164→     */
165→    @Synchronized
166→    fun setDryRun(dryRun: Boolean) {
167→        Timber.i("setDryRun(): %s, warning dryRun is experimental", dryRun)
168→    }
169→
170→    /**
171→     * Re-Initialize the analytics provider
172→     */
173→    @Synchronized
174→    fun reInitialize() {
175→        // send any pending async hits, re-chain default exception handlers and re-init
176→        Timber.i("reInitialize()")
177→        sAnalytics!!.flush()
178→        sAnalytics = null
179→        unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler()
180→        initialize(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext)
181→    }
182→
183→    /**
184→     * Submit a screen for aggregation / analysis.
185→     * Intended for use to determine if / how features are being used
186→     *
187→     * @param object the result of Object.getClass().getSimpleName() will be used as the screen tag
188→     */
189→    @KotlinCleanup("rename object")
190→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`: Any) {
191→        sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`.javaClass.simpleName)
192→    }
193→
194→    /**
195→     * Submit a screen display with a synthetic name for aggregation / analysis
196→     * Intended for use if your class handles multiple screens you want to track separately
197→     *
198→     * @param screenName screenName the name to show in analysis reports
199→     */
200→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(screenName: String) {
201→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsScreenView(): %s", screenName)
202→        if (!optIn) {
203→            return
204→        }
205→        sAnalytics!!.screenView().screenName(screenName).sendAsync()
206→    }
207→
208→    /**
209→     * Send a detailed arbitrary analytics event, with noun/verb pairs and extra data if needed
210→     *
211→     * @param category the category of event, make your own but use a constant so reporting is good
212→     * @param action   the action the user performed
213→     * @param value    A value for the event, Integer.MIN_VALUE signifies caller shouldn't send the value
214→     * @param label    A label for the event, may be null
215→     */
216→    fun sendAnalyticsEvent(category: String, action: String, value: Int? = null, label: String? = null) {
217→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsEvent() category/action/value/label: %s/%s/%s/%s", category, action, value, label)
218→        if (!optIn) {
219→            return
220→        }
221→        val event = sAnalytics!!.event().eventCategory(category).eventAction(action)
222→        if (label != null) {
223→            event.eventLabel(label)
224→        }
225→        if (value != null) {
226→            event.eventValue(value)
227→        }
228→        event.sendAsync()
229→    }
230→
231→    /**
232→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis, parsed from the exception information
233→     *
234→     * @param t     Throwable to send for analysis
235→     * @param fatal whether it was fatal or not
236→     */
237→    fun sendAnalyticsException(t: Throwable, fatal: Boolean) {
238→        sendAnalyticsException(getCause(t).toString(), fatal)
239→    }
240→
241→    @KotlinCleanup("convert to sequence")
242→    fun getCause(t: Throwable): Throwable {
243→        var cause: Throwable?
244→        var result = t
245→        while (null != result.cause.also { cause = it } && result != cause) {
246→            result = cause!!
247→        }
248→        return result
249→    }
250→
251→    /**
252→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis
253→     *
254→     * @param description API limited to 100 characters, truncated here to 100 if needed
255→     * @param fatal       whether it was fatal or not
256→     */
257→    fun sendAnalyticsException(description: String, fatal: Boolean) {
258→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsException() description/fatal: %s/%s", description, fatal)
259→        if (!sOptIn) {
260→            return
261→        }
262→        sAnalytics!!.exception().exceptionDescription(description).exceptionFatal(fatal).sendAsync()
263→    }
264→
265→    internal fun canGetDefaultUserAgent(): Boolean {
266→        // #5502 - getDefaultUserAgent starts a WebView. We can't have two WebViews with the same data directory.
267→        // But ACRA starts an :acra process which does not terminate when AnkiDroid is restarted. https://crbug.com/558377
268→
269→        // if we're not under the ACRA process then we're fine to initialize a WebView
270→        return if (!ACRA.isACRASenderServiceProcess()) {
271→            true
272→        } else {
273→            hasSetDataDirectory()
274→        }
275→
276→        // If we have a custom data directory, then the crash will not occur.
277→    }
278→
279→    // A listener on this preference handles the rest
280→    var isEnabled: Boolean
281→        get() {
282→            val userPrefs = AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs()
283→            return userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
284→        }
285→        set(value) {
286→            // A listener on this preference handles the rest
287→            AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs().edit {
288→                putBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, value)
289→            }
290→        }

292→    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
293→    fun resetForTests() {
294→        sAnalytics = null
295→    }
296→
297→    /**
298→     * An Android-specific device config generator. Without this it's "Desktop" and unknown for all hardware.
299→     * It is interesting to us what devices people use though (for instance: is Amazon Kindle support worth it?
300→     * Is anyone still using e-ink devices? How many people are on tablets? ChromeOS?)
301→     */
302→    private class AndroidDefaultRequest : DefaultRequest() {
303→        fun setAndroidRequestParameters(context: Context): DefaultRequest {
304→            // Are we running on really large screens or small screens? Send raw screen size
305→            try {
306→                val size = DisplayUtils.getDisplayDimensions(context)
307→                this.screenResolution(size.x.toString() + "x" + size.y)
308→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
309→                Timber.w(e)
310→                // nothing much to do here, it means we couldn't get WindowManager
311→            }
312→
313→            // We can have up to 20 of these - there might be other things we want to know
314→            // but simply seeing what hardware we are running on should be useful
315→            this.customDimension(1, Build.VERSION.RELEASE) // systemVersion, e.g. "7.1.1"  for Android 7.1.1
316→            this.customDimension(2, Build.BRAND) // brand e.g. "OnePlus"
317→            this.customDimension(3, Build.MODEL) // model e.g. "ONEPLUS A6013" for the 6T
318→            this.customDimension(4, Build.BOARD) // deviceId e.g. "sdm845" for the 6T
319→
320→            // This is important for google to auto-fingerprint us for default reporting
321→            // It is not possible to set operating system explicitly, there is no API or analytics parameter for it
322→            // Instead they respond that they auto-parse User-Agent strings for analytics attribution
323→            // For maximum analytics built-in report compatibility we will send the official WebView User-Agent string
324→            try {
325→                if (canGetDefaultUserAgent()) {
326→                    this.userAgent(WebSettings.getDefaultUserAgent(context))
327→                } else {
328→                    this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
329→                }
330→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
331→                Timber.w(e)
332→                // Catch RuntimeException as WebView initialization blows up in unpredictable ways
333→                // but analytics should never be a show-stopper
334→                this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
335→            }
336→            return this
337→        }
338→    }

 14→ * this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.                           *
 15→ ****************************************************************************************/
 16→
 17→/**
 18→ * With the Rust backend, operations that modify the collection return a description of changes (OpChanges).
 19→ * The UI can subscribe to these changes, so it can update itself when actions have been performed
 20→ * (eg, the deck list can check if studyQueues has been updated, and if so, it will redraw the list).
 21→ *
 22→ * The optional handler argument can be used so that the initiator of an action can tell when a
 23→ * OpChanges action was caused by itself. This can be useful when the default change behaviour
 24→ * should be ignored, in favour of specific handling (eg the UI wishes to directly update the
 25→ * displayed flag, without redrawing the entire review screen).
 26→ */
 27→
 28→package com.ichi2.libanki
 29→
 30→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 31→import anki.collection.OpChanges
 32→import anki.collection.OpChangesAfterUndo
 33→import anki.collection.OpChangesWithCount
 34→import anki.collection.OpChangesWithId
 35→import anki.import_export.ImportResponse
 36→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.withCol
 37→import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
 38→import kotlinx.coroutines.withContext
 39→import java.lang.ref.WeakReference
 40→
 41→object ChangeManager {
 42→    interface Subscriber {
 43→        /**
 44→         * Called after a backend method invoked via col.op() or col.opWithProgress()
 45→         * has modified the collection. Subscriber should inspect the changes, and update
 46→         * the UI if necessary.
 47→         */
 48→        fun opExecuted(changes: OpChanges, handler: Any?)
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    private val subscribers = mutableListOf<WeakReference<Subscriber>>()
 52→
 53→    fun subscribe(subscriber: Subscriber) {
 54→        subscribers.add(WeakReference(subscriber))
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    private fun notifySubscribers(changes: OpChanges, handler: Any?) {
 58→        val expired = mutableListOf<WeakReference<Subscriber>>()
 59→        for (subscriber in subscribers) {
 60→            val ref = subscriber.get()
 61→            if (ref == null) {
 62→                expired.add(subscriber)
 63→            } else {
 64→                ref.opExecuted(changes, handler)
 65→            }
 66→        }
 67→        for (item in expired) {
 68→            subscribers.remove(item)
 69→        }
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    @VisibleForTesting
 73→    fun clearSubscribers() {
 74→        subscribers.clear()
 75→    }
 76→
 77→    internal fun <T> notifySubscribers(changes: T, initiator: Any?) {
 78→        val opChanges = when (changes) {
 79→            is OpChanges -> changes
 80→            is OpChangesWithCount -> changes.changes
 81→            is OpChangesWithId -> changes.changes
 82→            is OpChangesAfterUndo -> changes.changes
 83→            is ImportResponse -> changes.changes
 84→            else -> TODO("unhandled change type")
 85→        }
 86→        notifySubscribers(opChanges, initiator)
 87→    }
 88→}
 89→
 90→/** Wrap a routine that returns OpChanges* or similar undo info with this
 91→ * to notify change subscribers of the changes. */
 92→suspend fun <T> undoableOp(handler: Any? = null, block: Collection.() -> T): T {
 93→    return withCol {
 94→        block()
 95→    }.also {
 96→        withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
 97→            ChangeManager.notifySubscribers(it, handler)
 98→        }
 99→    }
100→}
101→

13→package com.ichi2.ui
14→
15→import android.app.Activity
16→import android.content.Context
17→import android.content.ContextWrapper
18→import android.view.MenuItem
19→import android.view.View
20→import android.widget.ImageButton
21→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
22→import androidx.appcompat.widget.TooltipCompat
23→import com.ichi2.anki.ActionProviderCompat
24→import com.ichi2.anki.R
25→
26→/**
27→ * An Rtl version of a normal action view, where the drawable is mirrored
28→ */
29→class RtlCompliantActionProvider(context: Context) : ActionProviderCompat(context) {
30→    @VisibleForTesting
31→    val mActivity: Activity
32→
33→    /**
34→     * The action to perform when clicking the associated menu item. By default this delegates to
35→     * the activity's [Activity.onOptionsItemSelected] callback, but a custom action can be set and
36→     * used instead.
37→     */
38→    var clickHandler: (Activity, MenuItem) -> Unit = { activity, menuItem ->
39→        activity.onOptionsItemSelected(menuItem)
40→    }
41→
42→    override fun onCreateActionView(forItem: MenuItem): View {
43→        val actionView = ImageButton(context, null, android.R.attr.actionButtonStyle)
44→        TooltipCompat.setTooltipText(actionView, forItem.title)
45→        forItem.icon?.let {
46→            it.isAutoMirrored = true
47→            actionView.setImageDrawable(it)
48→        }
49→        actionView.id = R.id.action_undo
50→        actionView.setOnClickListener {
51→            if (!forItem.isEnabled) {
52→                return@setOnClickListener
53→            }
54→            clickHandler(mActivity, forItem)
55→        }
56→        return actionView
57→    }
58→
59→    companion object {
60→        /**
61→         * Unwrap a context to get the base activity back.
62→         * @param context a context that may be of type [ContextWrapper]
63→         * @return The activity of the passed context
64→         */
65→        private fun unwrapContext(context: Context): Activity {
66→            var unwrappedContext: Context? = context
67→            while (unwrappedContext !is Activity && unwrappedContext is ContextWrapper) {
68→                unwrappedContext = unwrappedContext.baseContext
69→            }
70→            return if (unwrappedContext is Activity) {
71→                unwrappedContext
72→            } else {
73→                throw ClassCastException("Passed context should be either an instanceof Activity or a ContextWrapper wrapping an Activity")
74→            }
75→        }
76→    }
77→
78→    init {
79→        mActivity = unwrapContext(context)
80→    }
81→}
82→

 18→package com.ichi2.anki

 20→import android.content.Context
 21→import android.view.View
 22→import android.widget.FrameLayout
 23→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 24→import androidx.recyclerview.widget.LinearLayoutManager
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.OnboardingUtils.Companion.addFeatures
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.OnboardingUtils.Companion.isVisited
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.OnboardingUtils.Companion.setVisited
 28→import com.ichi2.utils.HandlerUtils.executeFunctionUsingHandler
 29→
 30→/**
 31→ * Suppose a tutorial needs to be added for an activity called MyActivity.
 32→ * Steps for doing it:
 33→ *
 34→ * 1. If an inner class for MyActivity exists, then use it otherwise create a new class
 35→ * inside Onboarding and initialise an object of that class inside MyActivity. In this case,
 36→ * call onCreate() if the tutorial needs to be displayed when the screen is opened and does not have
 37→ * any particular time or view visibility on which it depends. If the class already exists,
 38→ * go to step 3.
 39→ *
 40→ * 2. If MyActivity does not already exist, then create it by extending Onboarding and
 41→ * then create a new enum class implementing OnboardingFlag.
 42→ *
 43→ * 3. Create a new method to display the tutorial.
 44→ *
 45→ * 4. Add the function using TutorialArgument data class to the list of tutorials in the init block
 46→ * if it has to be invoked which the screen is opened. If the function has to be invoked at a particular
 47→ * time, then call it from MyActivity.
 48→ *
 49→ * 5. For any extra condition that needs to be checked before displaying a tutorial, add it as the
 50→ * 'mOnboardingCondition' parameter in TutorialArguments.
 51→ */
 52→
 53→// Contains classes for various screens. Each sub-class has methods to show onboarding tutorials.
 54→// TODO: Move OnboardingUtils.featureConstants to a DI container rather than a mutable singleton
 55→abstract class Onboarding<Feature>(
 56→    private val context: Context,
 57→    val tutorials: MutableList<TutorialArguments<Feature>>
 58→) where Feature : Enum<Feature>, Feature : OnboardingFlag {
 59→
 60→    companion object {
 61→        // Constants being used for onboarding preferences should not be modified.
 62→        const val DECK_PICKER_ONBOARDING = "DeckPickerOnboarding"
 63→        const val REVIEWER_ONBOARDING = "ReviewerOnboarding"
 64→        const val NOTE_EDITOR_ONBOARDING = "NoteEditorOnboarding"
 65→        const val CARD_BROWSER_ONBOARDING = "CardBrowserOnboarding"
 66→
 67→        init {
 68→            addDefaultFeatures()
 69→        }
 70→
 71→        @VisibleForTesting
 72→        fun resetOnboardingForTesting() {
 73→            OnboardingUtils.featureConstants.clear()
 74→            addDefaultFeatures()
 75→        }
 76→
 77→        private fun addDefaultFeatures() {
 78→            addFeatures(
 79→                listOf(
 80→                    DECK_PICKER_ONBOARDING,
 81→                    REVIEWER_ONBOARDING,
 82→                    NOTE_EDITOR_ONBOARDING,
 83→                    CARD_BROWSER_ONBOARDING
 84→                )
 85→            )
 86→        }
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    /**
 90→     * Contains the logic for iterating through various tutorials of a screen and displaying the first one
 91→     * in the list which is not visited yet and condition (if any) also holds true for it.
 92→     *
 93→     */
 94→    fun onCreate() {
 95→        tutorials.forEach {
 96→            // If tutorial is visited or condition is false then return from the loop.
 97→            if (isVisited(it.featureIdentifier, context) || it.onboardingCondition?.invoke() == false) {
 98→                return@forEach
 99→            }
100→
101→            // Invoke the function to display the tutorial.
102→            it.onboardingFunction.invoke()
103→
104→            // Return so that other tutorials are not displayed.
105→            return
106→        }
107→    }
108→
109→    /**
110→     * Arguments required to handle the tutorial for a screen.
111→     *
112→     * @param featureIdentifier Enum constant for the feature.
113→     * @param onboardingFunction Function which has to be invoked if the tutorial needs to be displayed.
114→     * @param onboardingCondition Condition to be checked before displaying the tutorial. Tutorial should
115→     * be displayed only if mOnboardingCondition is not null and returns true. Default value is null
116→     * which indicates that no condition is required.
117→     */
118→    data class TutorialArguments<Feature>(
119→        val featureIdentifier: Feature,
120→        val onboardingFunction: () -> Unit,
121→        val onboardingCondition: (() -> Boolean)? = null
122→    )
123→            where Feature : Enum<Feature>, Feature : OnboardingFlag
124→
125→    class DeckPicker(
126→        private val activityContext: com.ichi2.anki.DeckPicker,
127→        private val recyclerViewLayoutManager: LinearLayoutManager
128→    ) : Onboarding<DeckPicker.DeckPickerOnboardingEnum>(activityContext, mutableListOf()) {
129→
130→        init {
131→            tutorials.add(TutorialArguments(DeckPickerOnboardingEnum.FAB, this::showTutorialForFABIfNew))
132→            tutorials.add(TutorialArguments(DeckPickerOnboardingEnum.DECK_NAME, this::showTutorialForDeckIfNew, activityContext::hasAtLeastOneDeckBeingDisplayed))
133→            tutorials.add(TutorialArguments(DeckPickerOnboardingEnum.COUNTS_LAYOUT, this::showTutorialForCountsLayoutIfNew, activityContext::hasAtLeastOneDeckBeingDisplayed))
134→        }
135→
136→        private fun showTutorialForFABIfNew() {
137→            CustomMaterialTapTargetPromptBuilder(activityContext, DeckPickerOnboardingEnum.FAB)
138→                .createCircleWithDimmedBackground()
139→                .setFocalColourResource(R.color.material_blue_500)
140→                .setDismissedListener { onCreate() }
141→                .setTarget(R.id.fab_main)
142→                .setPrimaryText(R.string.fab_tutorial_title)
143→                .setSecondaryText(R.string.fab_tutorial_desc)
144→                .show()
145→        }
146→
147→        private fun showTutorialForDeckIfNew() {
148→            CustomMaterialTapTargetPromptBuilder(activityContext, DeckPickerOnboardingEnum.DECK_NAME)
149→                .createRectangleWithDimmedBackground()
150→                .setDismissedListener { showTutorialForCountsLayoutIfNew() }
151→                .setTarget(recyclerViewLayoutManager.getChildAt(0)?.findViewById(R.id.deck_name_linear_layout))
152→                .setPrimaryText(R.string.start_studying)
153→                .setSecondaryText(R.string.start_studying_desc)
154→                .show()
155→        }
156→
157→        private fun showTutorialForCountsLayoutIfNew() {
158→            CustomMaterialTapTargetPromptBuilder(activityContext, DeckPickerOnboardingEnum.COUNTS_LAYOUT)
159→                .createRectangleWithDimmedBackground()
160→                .setTarget(recyclerViewLayoutManager.getChildAt(0)?.findViewById(R.id.counts_layout))
161→                .setPrimaryText(R.string.menu__study_options)
162→                .setSecondaryText(R.string.study_options_desc)
163→                .show()
164→        }
165→
166→        enum class DeckPickerOnboardingEnum(var value: Int) : OnboardingFlag {
167→            FAB(0), DECK_NAME(1), COUNTS_LAYOUT(2);
168→
169→            override fun getOnboardingEnumValue(): Int {
170→                return value
171→            }
172→
173→            override fun getFeatureConstant(): String {
174→                return DECK_PICKER_ONBOARDING
175→            }
176→        }
177→    }
178→
179→    class Reviewer(private val activityContext: com.ichi2.anki.Reviewer) :
180→        Onboarding<Reviewer.ReviewerOnboardingEnum>(activityContext, mutableListOf()) {
181→
182→        init {
183→            tutorials.add(TutorialArguments(ReviewerOnboardingEnum.SHOW_ANSWER, this::onQuestionShown))
184→            tutorials.add(TutorialArguments(ReviewerOnboardingEnum.FLAG, this::showTutorialForFlagIfNew))
185→        }
186→
187→        private fun onQuestionShown() {
188→            CustomMaterialTapTargetPromptBuilder(activityContext, ReviewerOnboardingEnum.SHOW_ANSWER)
189→                .createRectangleWithDimmedBackground()
190→                .setDismissedListener { onCreate() }
191→                // FIXME .setTarget(R.id.flip_card)
192→                .setPrimaryText(R.string.see_answer)
193→                .setSecondaryText(R.string.see_answer_desc)
194→                .show()
195→        }
196→
197→        /**
198→         * Called when the difficulty buttons are displayed after clicking on 'Show Answer'.
199→         */
200→        fun onAnswerShown() {
201→            if (isVisited(ReviewerOnboardingEnum.DIFFICULTY_RATING, activityContext)) {
202→                return
203→            }
204→
205→            CustomMaterialTapTargetPromptBuilder(activityContext, ReviewerOnboardingEnum.DIFFICULTY_RATING)
206→                .createRectangleWithDimmedBackground()
207→                .setTarget(R.id.ease_buttons)
208→                .setPrimaryText(R.string.select_difficulty)
209→                .setSecondaryText(R.string.select_difficulty_desc)
210→                .show()
211→        }
212→
213→        private fun showTutorialForFlagIfNew() {
214→            // Handler is required here to show feature prompt on menu items. Reference: https://github.com/sjwall/MaterialTapTargetPrompt/issues/73#issuecomment-320681655
215→            executeFunctionUsingHandler {
216→                CustomMaterialTapTargetPromptBuilder(activityContext, ReviewerOnboardingEnum.FLAG)
217→                    .createCircle()
218→                    .setFocalColourResource(R.color.material_blue_500)
219→                    .setTarget(R.id.action_flag)
220→                    .setPrimaryText(R.string.menu_flag_card)
221→                    .setSecondaryText(R.string.flag_card_desc)
222→                    .show()
223→            }
224→        }
225→
226→        /**
227→         * Show after undo button goes into enabled state
228→         */
229→        fun onUndoButtonEnabled() {
230→            if (isVisited(ReviewerOnboardingEnum.UNDO, activityContext)) {
231→                return
232→            }
233→
234→            CustomMaterialTapTargetPromptBuilder(activityContext, ReviewerOnboardingEnum.UNDO)
235→                .createCircleWithDimmedBackground()

237→                .setTarget(R.id.action_undo)
238→                .setPrimaryText(R.string.undo)
239→                .setSecondaryText(R.string.undo_desc)
240→                .show()
241→        }
242→
243→        enum class ReviewerOnboardingEnum(var value: Int) : OnboardingFlag {
244→            SHOW_ANSWER(0), DIFFICULTY_RATING(1), FLAG(2), UNDO(3);
245→
246→            override fun getOnboardingEnumValue(): Int {
247→                return value
248→            }
249→
250→            override fun getFeatureConstant(): String {
251→                return REVIEWER_ONBOARDING
252→            }
253→        }
254→    }

content.text:
I have implemented the "Redo" feature in the reviewer, matching the functionality available in Anki Desktop. 

Key changes include:
1.  **Backend Support**: Added `redoAvailable()` and `redoLabel()` to the `Collection` class to expose redo status and action descriptions from the Rust backend.
2.  **Logic Implementation**: Added `redoAndShowSnackbar()` in `Undo.kt` to handle the redo operation and provide user feedback via a snackbar, similar to the existing undo functionality.
3.  **UI Integration**:
    *   Added a `redo()` method to `AbstractFlashcardViewer` and handled the `ViewerCommand.REDO` command.
    *   Updated `Reviewer.kt` to handle the redo menu item click and dynamically update the button's enabled state, icon, and title (e.g., "Redo Bury").
    *   Ensured the action bar refreshes after any operation to keep undo/redo buttons up to date.
4.  **Action Provider Fix**: Fixed a bug in `RtlCompliantActionProvider` where the action view ID was hardcoded to `action_undo`, which would have prevented the redo button from working correctly when clicked.
5.  **Commands & Bindings**: Added `REDO` to `ViewerCommand` with default keyboard shortcuts: `Ctrl+Y` and `Ctrl+Shift+Z`.
6.  **Configuration & Analytics**: 
    *   Registered the redo button in `ActionButtonStatus` for custom button management.
    *   Added redo-related preferences to the reset list in `CustomButtonsSettingsFragment`.
    *   Updated `UsageAnalytics` to track redo button usage and keybindings.
7.  **Resources**: Created the missing `ic_redo.xml` vector drawable.

These changes provide a complete redo experience in the AnkiDroid reviewer, allowing users to easily recover from accidental undos.

🤖 Generated with [Firebender](https://firebender.com)

Co-Authored-By: Firebender <help@firebender.com>

17→package com.ichi2.anki
18→
19→import androidx.fragment.app.FragmentActivity
20→import anki.collection.OpChangesAfterUndo
21→import com.google.android.material.snackbar.Snackbar
22→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.TR
23→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
24→import com.ichi2.libanki.undo
25→import com.ichi2.libanki.undoableOp
26→
27→/** If there's an action pending in the review queue, undo it and show a snackbar */
28→suspend fun FragmentActivity.undoAndShowSnackbar(duration: Int = Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT) {
29→    withProgress {
30→        val changes = undoableOp {
31→            if (!undoAvailable()) {
32→                OpChangesAfterUndo.getDefaultInstance()
33→            } else {
34→                undo()
35→            }
36→        }
37→        val message = if (changes.operation.isEmpty()) {
38→            TR.actionsNothingToUndo()
39→        } else {
40→            TR.undoActionUndone(changes.operation)
41→        }
42→        showSnackbar(message, duration)
43→    }
44→}

 50→// Anki maintains a cache of used tags so it can quickly present a list of tags
 51→// for autocomplete and in the browser. For efficiency, deletions are not
 52→// tracked, so unused tags can only be removed from the list with a DB check.
 53→//
 54→// This module manages the tag cache and tags for notes.
 55→@KotlinCleanup("TextUtils -> Kotlin isNotEmpty()")
 56→@KotlinCleanup("inline function in init { } so we don't need to init `crt` etc... at the definition")

 59→open class Collection(
 60→    /**
 61→     *  @param Path The path to the collection.anki2 database. Must be unicode and openable with [File].
 62→     */

183→    fun schedVer(): Int {
184→        // schedVer was not set on legacy v1 collections
185→        val ver = config.get("schedVer") ?: 1
186→        return if (listOf(1, 2).contains(ver)) {
187→            ver
188→        } else {
189→            throw RuntimeException("Unsupported scheduler version")
190→        }

193→    fun _loadScheduler() {
194→        val ver = schedVer()
195→        if (ver == 1) {
196→            sched = DummyScheduler(this)
197→        } else {
198→            if (!backend.getConfigBool(ConfigKey.Bool.SCHED_2021)) {
199→                backend.setConfigBool(ConfigKey.Bool.SCHED_2021, true, undoable = false)
200→            }
201→            sched = Scheduler(this)
202→            config.set("localOffset", sched._current_timezone_offset())
203→        }
204→    }

206→    /**
207→     * Disconnect from DB.
208→     * Python implementation has a save argument for legacy reasons;
209→     * AnkiDroid always saves as changes are made.
210→     */
211→    @Synchronized
212→    fun close(downgrade: Boolean = false, forFullSync: Boolean = false) {
213→        if (!dbClosed) {
214→            if (!forFullSync) {
215→                backend.closeCollection(downgrade)
216→            }
217→            dbInternal = null
218→            _closeLog()
219→            Timber.i("Collection closed")
220→        }
221→    }

223→    /** True if DB was created */
224→    fun reopen(afterFullSync: Boolean = false): Boolean {
225→        Timber.i("(Re)opening Database: %s", path)
226→        return if (dbClosed) {
227→            val (db_, created) = Storage.openDB(path, backend, afterFullSync)
228→            dbInternal = db_
229→            load()
230→            _openLog()
231→            if (afterFullSync) {
232→                _loadScheduler()
233→            }
234→            created
235→        } else {
236→            false
237→        }
238→    }

246→    /** Note: not in libanki.  Mark schema modified to force a full

248→     * is equivalent to `modSchema(False)` in Anki. A distinct method
249→     * is used so that the type does not states that an exception is
250→     * thrown when in fact it is never thrown.
251→     */
252→    open fun modSchemaNoCheck() {
253→        db.execute(
254→            "update col set scm=?, mod=?",
255→            TimeManager.time.intTimeMS(),
256→            TimeManager.time.intTimeMS()
257→        )

260→    /** Mark schema modified to force a full sync.
261→     * ConfirmModSchemaException will be thrown if the user needs to be prompted to confirm the action.
262→     * If the user chooses to confirm then modSchemaNoCheck should be called, after which the exception can
263→     * be safely ignored, and the outer code called again.
264→     *
265→     * @throws ConfirmModSchemaException
266→     */
267→    @Throws(ConfirmModSchemaException::class)
268→    fun modSchema() {
269→        if (!schemaChanged()) {
270→            /* In Android we can't show a dialog which blocks the main UI thread
271→             Therefore we can't wait for the user to confirm if they want to do
272→             a full sync here, and we instead throw an exception asking the outer
273→             code to handle the user's choice */
274→            throw ConfirmModSchemaException()
275→        }
276→        modSchemaNoCheck()
277→    }

373→    fun findCards(
374→        search: String,
375→        order: SortOrder
376→    ): List<Long> {
377→        val adjustedOrder = if (order is SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering) {
378→            SortOrder.BuiltinSortKind(
379→                config.get("sortType") ?: "noteFld",
380→                config.get("sortBackwards") ?: false
381→            )
382→        } else {
383→            order
384→        }
385→        val cardIdsList = try {
386→            backend.searchCards(search, adjustedOrder.toProtoBuf())
387→        } catch (e: BackendInvalidInputException) {
388→            throw InvalidSearchException(e)
389→        }
390→        return cardIdsList
391→    }

398→            SortOrder.BuiltinSortKind(
399→                config.get("noteSortType") ?: "noteFld",
400→                config.get("browserNoteSortBackwards") ?: false
401→            )

420→    @RustCleanup("Remove in V16.") // Not in libAnki
421→    fun findOneCardByNote(query: String): List<CardId> {
422→        // This function shouldn't exist and CardBrowser should be modified to use Notes,
423→        // so not much effort was expended here
424→
425→        val noteIds = findNotes(query, SortOrder.NoOrdering())
426→        // select the card with the lowest `ord` to show
427→        return db.queryLongList(
428→            """
429→SELECT c.id
430→FROM (
431→  SELECT nid, MIN(ord) AS ord
432→  FROM cards
433→  WHERE nid IN ${Utils.ids2str(noteIds)} 
434→  GROUP BY nid
435→) AS card_with_min_ord
436→JOIN cards AS c ON card_with_min_ord.nid = c.nid AND card_with_min_ord.ord = c.ord;
437→            """.trimMargin()
438→        )
439→    }

457→    fun startTimebox() {
458→        mStartTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
459→        mStartReps = sched.reps
460→    }
461→
462→    data class TimeboxReached(val secs: Int, val reps: Int)
463→
464→    /* Return (elapsedTime, reps) if timebox reached, or null.
465→    * Automatically restarts timebox if expired. */
466→    fun timeboxReached(): TimeboxReached? {
467→        if (sched.timeboxSecs() == 0) {
468→            // timeboxing disabled
469→            return null
470→        }
471→        val elapsed = TimeManager.time.intTime() - mStartTime
472→        val limit = sched.timeboxSecs()
473→        return if (elapsed > limit) {
474→            TimeboxReached(
475→                limit,
476→                sched.reps - mStartReps
477→            ).also {
478→                startTimebox()
479→            }
480→        } else {
481→            null
482→        }
483→    }

536→    /*
537→     * Basic integrity check. Only used by unit tests.
538→     */
539→    @KotlinCleanup("have getIds() return a list of mids and define idsToStr over it")
540→    fun basicCheck(): Boolean {
541→        // cards without notes
542→        if (db.queryScalar("select 1 from cards where nid not in (select id from notes) limit 1") > 0) {
543→            return false
544→        }
545→        val badNotes = db.queryScalar(
546→            "select 1 from notes where id not in (select distinct nid from cards) " +
547→                "or mid not in " + Utils.ids2str(notetypes.ids()) + " limit 1"
548→        ) > 0
549→        // notes without cards or models
550→        if (badNotes) {
551→            return false
552→        }
553→        // invalid ords
554→        for (m in notetypes.all()) {
555→            // ignore clozes
556→            if (m.getInt("type") != Consts.MODEL_STD) {
557→                continue
558→            }
559→            // Make a list of valid ords for this model
560→            val tmpls = m.getJSONArray("tmpls")
561→            val badOrd = db.queryScalar(
562→                "select 1 from cards where (ord < 0 or ord >= ?) and nid in ( " +
563→                    "select id from notes where mid = ?) limit 1",
564→                tmpls.length(),
565→                m.getLong("id")
566→            ) > 0
567→            if (badOrd) {
568→                return false
569→            }
570→        }
571→        return true
572→    }

574→    fun log(vararg objects: Any?) {
575→        if (!debugLog) return
576→
577→        val unixTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
578→
579→        val outerTraceElement = Thread.currentThread().stackTrace[3]
580→        val fileName = outerTraceElement.fileName
581→        val methodName = outerTraceElement.methodName
582→
583→        val objectsString = objects
584→            .map { if (it is LongArray) Arrays.toString(it) else it }
585→            .joinToString(", ")
586→
587→        writeLog("[$unixTime] $fileName:$methodName() $objectsString")
588→    }
589→
590→    private fun writeLog(s: String) {
591→        mLogHnd?.let {
592→            try {
593→                it.println(s)
594→            } catch (e: Exception) {
595→                Timber.w(e, "Failed to write to collection log")
596→            }
597→        }
598→        Timber.d(s)
599→    }
600→
601→    private fun _openLog() {
602→        Timber.i("Opening Collection Log")
603→        if (!debugLog) {
604→            return
605→        }
606→        try {
607→            val lpath = File(path.replaceFirst("\\.anki2$".toRegex(), ".log"))
608→            if (lpath.exists() && lpath.length() > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
609→                val lpath2 = File("$lpath.old")
610→                if (lpath2.exists()) {
611→                    lpath2.delete()
612→                }
613→                lpath.renameTo(lpath2)
614→            }
615→            mLogHnd = PrintWriter(BufferedWriter(FileWriter(lpath, true)), true)
616→        } catch (e: IOException) {
617→            // turn off logging if we can't open the log file
618→            Timber.e("Failed to open collection.log file - disabling logging")
619→            debugLog = false
620→        }
621→    }
622→
623→    private fun _closeLog() {
624→        Timber.i("Closing Collection Log")
625→        mLogHnd?.close()
626→        mLogHnd = null
627→    }

17→package com.ichi2.libanki
18→
19→import anki.collection.OpChangesAfterUndo
20→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.RustCleanup
21→import anki.collection.UndoStatus as UndoStatusProto
22→
23→/**
24→ * If undo/redo available, a localized string describing the action will be set.
25→ */
26→data class UndoStatus(
27→    val undo: String?,
28→    val redo: String?,
29→    // not currently used
30→    val lastStep: Int
31→) {
32→    companion object {
33→        fun from(proto: UndoStatusProto): UndoStatus {
34→            return UndoStatus(
35→                undo = proto.undo.ifEmpty { null },
36→                redo = proto.redo.ifEmpty { null },
37→                lastStep = proto.lastStep
38→            )
39→        }
40→    }
41→}
42→
43→/**
44→ * Undo the last backend operation.
45→ *
46→ * Should be called via collection.op(), which will notify
47→ * [ChangeManager.Subscriber] of the changes.
48→ *
49→ * Will throw if no undo operation is possible (due to legacy code
50→ * directly mutating the database).
51→ */
52→@RustCleanup("Once fully migrated, and v2 scheduler dropped, rename to undo()")
53→fun Collection.undo(): OpChangesAfterUndo {
54→    return backend.undo()
55→}
56→
57→/** Redoes the previously-undone operation. See the docs for
58→[Collection.undoOperation]
59→ */
60→fun Collection.redo(): OpChangesAfterUndo {
61→    return backend.redo()
62→}
63→
64→/** See [UndoStatus] */
65→fun Collection.undoStatus(): UndoStatus {
66→    return UndoStatus.from(backend.getUndoStatus())
67→}

 199→    override fun shouldDisplayMark(): Boolean {
 200→        val markValue = super.shouldDisplayMark()
 201→        if (!markValue) {
 202→            return false
 203→        }
 204→
 205→        // If we don't know: assume it's not shown
 206→        val shownAsToolbarButton = mActionButtons.findMenuItem(ActionButtons.RES_MARK)?.isActionButton == true

 210→    protected open fun onMark(card: Card?) {

 217→            onMarkChanged()

 228→    protected open fun onFlag(card: Card?, flag: Int) {
 229→        if (card == null) {
 230→            return
 231→        }
 232→        launchCatchingTask {
 233→            card.setUserFlag(flag)
 234→            withCol {
 235→                setUserFlagForCards(listOf(card.id), flag)
 236→            }
 237→            refreshActionBar()
 238→            onFlagChanged()
 239→        }
 240→    }

 249→    private fun selectDeckFromExtra() {
 250→        val extras = intent.extras
 251→        if (extras == null || !extras.containsKey("deckId")) {
 252→            // deckId is not set, load default
 253→            return
 254→        }
 255→        val did = extras.getLong("deckId", Long.MIN_VALUE)
 256→        Timber.d("selectDeckFromExtra() with deckId = %d", did)
 257→
 258→        // deckId does not exist, load default
 259→        if (getColUnsafe.decks.get(did) == null) {
 260→            Timber.w("selectDeckFromExtra() deckId '%d' doesn't exist", did)
 261→            return
 262→        }
 263→        // Select the deck
 264→        getColUnsafe.decks.select(did)
 265→    }

 279→    override fun onCollectionLoaded(col: Collection) {
 280→        super.onCollectionLoaded(col)
 281→        if (Intent.ACTION_VIEW == intent.action) {
 282→            Timber.d("onCreate() :: received Intent with action = %s", intent.action)
 283→            selectDeckFromExtra()
 284→        }
 285→        // Load the first card and start reviewing. Uses the answer card
 286→        // task to load a card, but since we send null
 287→        // as the card to answer, no card will be answered.
 288→        prefWhiteboard = MetaDB.getWhiteboardState(this, parentDid)
 289→        if (prefWhiteboard) {
 290→            // DEFECT: Slight inefficiency here, as we set the database using these methods
 291→            val whiteboardVisibility = MetaDB.getWhiteboardVisibility(this, parentDid)
 292→            setWhiteboardEnabledState(true)
 293→            setWhiteboardVisibility(whiteboardVisibility)
 294→            toggleStylus = MetaDB.getWhiteboardStylusState(this, parentDid)
 295→            whiteboard!!.toggleStylus = toggleStylus
 296→        }
 297→        launchCatchingTask { updateCardAndRedraw() }
 298→        disableDrawerSwipeOnConflicts()
 299→
 300→        // Set full screen/immersive mode if needed
 301→        if (mPrefFullscreenReview) {
 302→            setFullScreen(this)
 303→        }
 304→        setRenderWorkaround(this)
 305→    }

 307→    override fun onOptionsItemSelected(item: MenuItem): Boolean {
 308→        // 100ms was not enough on my device (Honor 9 Lite -  Android Pie)
 309→        delayedHide(1000)
 310→        if (drawerToggle.onOptionsItemSelected(item)) {
 311→            return true
 312→        }
 313→        when (item.itemId) {
 314→            android.R.id.home -> {
 315→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Home button pressed")
 316→                closeReviewer(RESULT_OK)
 317→            }
 318→            R.id.action_undo -> {
 319→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Undo button pressed")
 320→                if (mShowWhiteboard && whiteboard != null && !whiteboard!!.undoEmpty()) {
 321→                    whiteboard!!.undo()
 322→                } else {
 323→                    undo()
 324→                }
 325→            }
 326→            R.id.action_reset_card_progress -> {
 327→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Reset progress button pressed")
 328→                showResetCardDialog()
 329→            }
 330→            R.id.action_mark_card -> {
 331→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Mark button pressed")
 332→                onMark(currentCard)
 333→            }
 334→            R.id.action_replay -> {
 335→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Replay audio button pressed (from menu)")
 336→                playSounds(true)
 337→            }
 338→            R.id.action_toggle_mic_tool_bar -> {
 339→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Record mic")
 340→                // Check permission to record and request if not granted
 341→                openOrToggleMicToolbar()
 342→            }
 343→            R.id.action_tag -> {
 344→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Tag button pressed")
 345→                showTagsDialog()
 346→            }
 347→            R.id.action_edit -> {
 348→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Edit note button pressed")
 349→                editCard()
 350→            }
 351→            R.id.action_bury -> {
 352→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Bury button pressed")
 353→                MenuItemCompat.getActionProvider(item)?.hasSubMenu()?.let { isAvailable ->
 354→                    if (!isAvailable) {
 355→                        Timber.d("Bury card due to no submenu")
 356→                        buryCard()
 357→                    }
 358→                } ?: Timber.w("Null ActionProvider for bury menu item in Reviewer!")
 359→            }
 360→            R.id.action_suspend -> {
 361→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Suspend button pressed")
 362→                MenuItemCompat.getActionProvider(item)?.hasSubMenu()?.let { isAvailable ->
 363→                    if (!isAvailable) {
 364→                        Timber.d("Suspend card due to no submenu")
 365→                        suspendCard()
 366→                    }
 367→                } ?: Timber.w("Null ActionProvider for suspend menu item in Reviewer!")
 368→            }
 369→            R.id.action_delete -> {
 370→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Delete note button pressed")
 371→                showDeleteNoteDialog()
 372→            }
 373→            R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color -> {
 374→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Pen Color button pressed")
 375→                changeWhiteboardPenColor()
 376→            }
 377→            R.id.action_save_whiteboard -> {
 378→                Timber.i("Reviewer:: Save whiteboard button pressed")
 379→                if (whiteboard != null) {
 380→                    try {
 381→                        val savedWhiteboardFileName = whiteboard!!.saveWhiteboard(TimeManager.time).path
 382→                        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.white_board_image_saved, savedWhiteboardFileName), Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT)
 383→                    } catch (e: Exception) {

 385→                        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.white_board_image_save_failed, e.localizedMessage), Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT)
 386→                    }
 387→                }

 389→            R.id.action_clear_whiteboard -> {

 393→            R.id.action_hide_whiteboard -> { // toggle whiteboard visibility

 463→    public override fun toggleWhiteboard() {
 464→        prefWhiteboard = !prefWhiteboard
 465→        Timber.i("Reviewer:: Whiteboard enabled state set to %b", prefWhiteboard)
 466→        // Even though the visibility is now stored in its own setting, we want it to be dependent
 467→        // on the enabled status
 468→        setWhiteboardEnabledState(prefWhiteboard)
 469→        setWhiteboardVisibility(prefWhiteboard)
 470→        if (!prefWhiteboard) {
 471→            mColorPalette.visibility = View.GONE
 472→        }
 473→        refreshActionBar()
 474→    }

 528→    override fun closeReviewer(result: Int) {
 529→        // Stop the mic recording if still pending
 530→        audioView?.notifyStopRecord()
 531→
 532→        // Remove the temporary audio file
 533→        tempAudioPath?.let {
 534→            val tempAudioPathToDelete = File(it)
 535→            if (tempAudioPathToDelete.exists()) {
 536→                tempAudioPathToDelete.delete()
 537→            }
 538→        }
 539→        super.closeReviewer(result)
 540→    }

 554→    private fun openOrToggleMicToolbar() {
 555→        if (!canRecordAudio(this)) {
 556→            ActivityCompat.requestPermissions(
 557→                this,
 558→                arrayOf(Manifest.permission.RECORD_AUDIO),

 566→    private fun toggleMicToolBar() {
 567→        audioView?.let {
 568→            it.visibility = if (it.visibility != View.VISIBLE) View.VISIBLE else View.GONE
 569→            return
 570→        }
 571→        // Record mic tool bar does not exist yet
 572→        tempAudioPath = generateTempAudioFile(this)
 573→        if (tempAudioPath == null) {
 574→            return
 575→        }
 576→        audioView = createRecorderInstance(
 577→            this,
 578→            R.drawable.ic_play_arrow_white_24dp,
 579→            R.drawable.ic_pause_white_24dp,
 580→            R.drawable.ic_stop_white_24dp,
 581→            R.drawable.ic_rec,
 582→            R.drawable.ic_rec_stop,
 583→            tempAudioPath!!
 584→        )
 585→        if (audioView == null) {
 586→            tempAudioPath = null
 587→            return
 588→        }
 589→        val lp2 = FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
 590→            ViewGroup.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
 591→            ViewGroup.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
 592→        )
 593→        audioView!!.layoutParams = lp2
 594→        val micToolBarLayer = findViewById<LinearLayout>(R.id.mic_tool_bar_layer)
 595→        micToolBarLayer.addView(audioView)
 596→    }

 661→    override fun onCreateOptionsMenu(menu: Menu): Boolean {
 662→        Timber.d("onCreateOptionsMenu()")
 663→        // NOTE: This is called every time a new question is shown via invalidate options menu
 664→        menuInflater.inflate(R.menu.reviewer, menu)
 665→        displayIcons(menu)
 666→        mActionButtons.setCustomButtonsStatus(menu)
 667→        var alpha = if (super.controlBlocked !== ReviewerUi.ControlBlock.SLOW) Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT else Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 668→        val markCardIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_mark_card)

 672→            markCardIcon.setTitle(R.string.menu_mark_note).setIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_border_white)
 673→        }
 674→        markCardIcon.iconAlpha = alpha
 675→
 676→        val flagIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_flag)
 677→        if (flagIcon != null) {
 678→            if (currentCard != null) {
 679→                when (currentCard!!.userFlag()) {
 680→                    1 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_red)
 681→                    2 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_orange)
 682→                    3 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_green)
 683→                    4 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_blue)
 684→                    5 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_pink)
 685→                    6 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_turquoise)
 686→                    7 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_purple)
 687→                    else -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_transparent)
 688→                }
 689→            }
 690→            flagIcon.iconAlpha = alpha
 691→        }
 692→
 693→        // Undo button
 694→        @DrawableRes val undoIconId: Int
 695→        val undoEnabled: Boolean
 696→        val whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes = mShowWhiteboard && whiteboard?.undoEmpty() == false
 697→        if (whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes) {
 698→            undoIconId = R.drawable.eraser
 699→            undoEnabled = true
 700→        } else {
 701→            undoIconId = R.drawable.ic_undo_white
 702→            undoEnabled = colIsOpenUnsafe() && getColUnsafe.undoAvailable()
 703→        }
 704→        val alphaUndo = if (undoEnabled && super.controlBlocked !== ReviewerUi.ControlBlock.SLOW) Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT else Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 705→        val undoIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_undo)
 706→        undoIcon.setIcon(undoIconId)
 707→        undoIcon.setEnabled(undoEnabled).iconAlpha = alphaUndo
 708→        undoIcon.actionView!!.isEnabled = undoEnabled

 710→            if (whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes) {

 715→            } else {

 717→                // so in some languages such as Japanese, which have pre/post-positional particle with the object,
 718→                // we need to use the string for just "Undo" instead of the string for "Undo %s".

 937→    private fun updateScreenCounts() {
 938→        val queue = queueState ?: return
 939→        super.updateActionBar()
 940→        val actionBar = supportActionBar
 941→        val counts = queue.counts
 942→        if (actionBar != null) {
 943→            if (mPrefShowETA) {
 944→                launchCatchingTask {
 945→                    mEta = withCol { sched.eta(counts, false) }
 946→                    actionBar.subtitle = remainingTime(this@Reviewer, (mEta * 60).toLong())
 947→                }
 948→            }
 949→        }
 950→        mNewCount = SpannableString(counts.new.toString())
 951→        mLrnCount = SpannableString(counts.lrn.toString())
 952→        mRevCount = SpannableString(counts.rev.toString())
 953→        if (mPrefHideDueCount) {
 954→            mRevCount = SpannableString("???")
 955→        }
 956→        // if this code is run as a card is being answered, currentCard may be non-null but
 957→        // the queues may be empty - we can't call countIdx() in such a case
 958→        if (counts.count() != 0) {
 959→            when (queue.countsIndex) {
 960→                Counts.Queue.NEW -> mNewCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mNewCount!!.length, 0)
 961→                Counts.Queue.LRN -> mLrnCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mLrnCount!!.length, 0)
 962→                Counts.Queue.REV -> mRevCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mRevCount!!.length, 0)
 963→            }
 964→        }
 965→        mTextBarNew.text = mNewCount
 966→        mTextBarLearn.text = mLrnCount
 967→        mTextBarReview.text = mRevCount
 968→    }

 997→    override suspend fun answerCardInner(ease: Int) {
 998→        val state = queueState!!
 999→        Timber.d("answerCardInner: ${currentCard!!.id} $ease")
1000→        var wasLeech = false
1001→        undoableOp(this) {
1002→            sched.answerCard(state, ease).also {
1003→                wasLeech = sched.againIsLeech(state)
1004→            }
1005→        }.also {
1006→            if (ease == Consts.BUTTON_ONE && wasLeech) {
1007→                state.topCard.load()
1008→                val leechMessage: String = if (state.topCard.queue < 0) {
1009→                    resources.getString(R.string.leech_suspend_notification)
1010→                } else {
1011→                    resources.getString(R.string.leech_notification)
1012→                }
1013→                showSnackbar(leechMessage, Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT)
1014→            }
1015→        }
1016→    }

1018→    private fun dealWithTimeBox(timebox: Collection.TimeboxReached) {
1019→        val nCards = timebox.reps
1020→        val nMins = timebox.secs / 60
1021→        val mins = resources.getQuantityString(R.plurals.in_minutes, nMins, nMins)
1022→        val timeboxMessage = resources.getQuantityString(R.plurals.timebox_reached, nCards, nCards, mins)
1023→        AlertDialog.Builder(this).show {
1024→            title(R.string.timebox_reached_title)
1025→            message(text = timeboxMessage)
1026→            positiveButton(R.string.dialog_continue) {}
1027→            negativeButton(text = CollectionManager.TR.studyingFinish()) {
1028→                finishWithAnimation(ActivityTransitionAnimation.Direction.END)
1029→            }
1030→            cancelable(true)
1031→            setOnCancelListener { }
1032→        }
1033→    }

1048→    private fun runStateMutationHook() {
1049→        val state = queueState ?: return
1050→        if (state.customSchedulingJs.isEmpty()) {
1051→            return
1052→        }
1053→        val key = customSchedulingKey
1054→        val js = state.customSchedulingJs
1055→        webView?.evaluateJavascript(
1056→            """
1057→        anki.mutateNextCardStates('$key', async (states, customData, ctx) => {{ $js }})
1058→            .catch(err => console.log(err));
1059→"""
1060→        ) {}
1061→    }

1063→    override fun initLayout() {

1106→    override fun executeCommand(which: ViewerCommand, fromGesture: Gesture?): Boolean {
1107→        if (isControlBlocked && which !== ViewerCommand.EXIT) {
1108→            return false
1109→        }
1110→        when (which) {
1111→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> {
1112→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_RED)
1113→                return true
1114→            }
1115→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> {
1116→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_ORANGE)
1117→                return true
1118→            }
1119→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> {
1120→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_GREEN)
1121→                return true
1122→            }
1123→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> {
1124→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_BLUE)
1125→                return true
1126→            }
1127→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> {
1128→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_PINK)
1129→                return true
1130→            }
1131→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> {
1132→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_TURQUOISE)
1133→                return true
1134→            }
1135→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> {
1136→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_PURPLE)
1137→                return true
1138→            }
1139→            ViewerCommand.UNSET_FLAG -> {
1140→                onFlag(currentCard, CardMarker.FLAG_NONE)
1141→                return true
1142→            }
1143→            ViewerCommand.MARK -> {
1144→                onMark(currentCard)
1145→                return true
1146→            }
1147→            ViewerCommand.ADD_NOTE -> {
1148→                addNote(fromGesture)
1149→                return true
1150→            }
1151→            ViewerCommand.CARD_INFO -> {
1152→                openCardInfo(fromGesture)
1153→                return true
1154→            }
1155→            ViewerCommand.RESCHEDULE_NOTE -> {
1156→                showRescheduleCardDialog()
1157→                return true
1158→            }
1159→            else -> return super.executeCommand(which, fromGesture)
1160→        }

1227→    @Suppress("deprecation") // #9332: UI Visibility -> Insets
1228→    private fun setFullScreen(a: AbstractFlashcardViewer) {
1229→        // Set appropriate flags to enable Sticky Immersive mode.
1230→        a.window.decorView.systemUiVisibility = (
1231→            View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_STABLE // | View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_HIDE_NAVIGATION // temporarily disabled due to #5245
1232→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_FULLSCREEN
1233→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_HIDE_NAVIGATION
1234→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_FULLSCREEN
1235→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LOW_PROFILE
1236→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_IMMERSIVE
1237→            )
1238→        // Show / hide the Action bar together with the status bar
1239→        val prefs = a.sharedPrefs()
1240→        val fullscreenMode = fromPreference(prefs)
1241→        a.window.statusBarColor = MaterialColors.getColor(a, R.attr.appBarColor, 0)
1242→        val decorView = a.window.decorView
1243→        decorView.setOnSystemUiVisibilityChangeListener { flags: Int ->
1244→            val toolbar = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.toolbar)
1245→            val answerButtons = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.answer_options_layout)
1246→            val topbar = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.top_bar)
1247→            if (toolbar == null || topbar == null || answerButtons == null) {
1248→                return@setOnSystemUiVisibilityChangeListener
1249→            }
1250→            // Note that system bars will only be "visible" if none of the
1251→            // LOW_PROFILE, HIDE_NAVIGATION, or FULLSCREEN flags are set.
1252→            val visible = flags and View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_HIDE_NAVIGATION == 0
1253→            Timber.d("System UI visibility change. Visible: %b", visible)
1254→            if (visible) {
1255→                showViewWithAnimation(toolbar)
1256→                if (fullscreenMode == FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE) {
1257→                    showViewWithAnimation(topbar)
1258→                    showViewWithAnimation(answerButtons)
1259→                }
1260→            } else {
1261→                hideViewWithAnimation(toolbar)
1262→                if (fullscreenMode == FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE) {
1263→                    hideViewWithAnimation(topbar)
1264→                    hideViewWithAnimation(answerButtons)
1265→                }
1266→            }
1267→        }
1268→    }
1269→
1270→    private fun showViewWithAnimation(view: View) {
1271→        view.alpha = 0.0f
1272→        view.visibility = View.VISIBLE
1273→        view.animate().alpha(TRANSPARENCY).setDuration(ANIMATION_DURATION.toLong()).setListener(null)
1274→    }
1275→
1276→    private fun hideViewWithAnimation(view: View) {
1277→        view.animate()
1278→            .alpha(0f)
1279→            .setDuration(ANIMATION_DURATION.toLong())
1280→            .setListener(object : AnimatorListenerAdapter() {
1281→                override fun onAnimationEnd(animation: Animator) {
1282→                    view.visibility = View.GONE
1283→                }
1284→            })
1285→    }

1292→    private fun createWhiteboard() {
1293→        whiteboard = createInstance(this, true, this)
1294→
1295→        // We use the pen color of the selected deck at the time the whiteboard is enabled.
1296→        // This is how all other whiteboard settings are
1297→        val whiteboardPenColor = MetaDB.getWhiteboardPenColor(this, parentDid).fromPreferences()
1298→        if (whiteboardPenColor != null) {
1299→            whiteboard!!.penColor = whiteboardPenColor
1300→        }
1301→        whiteboard!!.setOnPaintColorChangeListener(object : OnPaintColorChangeListener {
1302→            override fun onPaintColorChange(color: Int?) {
1303→                MetaDB.storeWhiteboardPenColor(this@Reviewer, parentDid, !currentTheme.isNightMode, color)
1304→            }
1305→        })
1306→        whiteboard!!.setOnTouchListener { v: View, event: MotionEvent? ->
1307→            if (event == null) return@setOnTouchListener false
1308→            // If the whiteboard is currently drawing, and triggers the system UI to show, we want to continue drawing.
1309→            if (!whiteboard!!.isCurrentlyDrawing && (
1310→                !mShowWhiteboard || (
1311→                    mPrefFullscreenReview &&
1312→                        isImmersiveSystemUiVisible(this@Reviewer)
1313→                    )
1314→                )
1315→            ) {
1316→                // Bypass whiteboard listener when it's hidden or fullscreen immersive mode is temporarily suspended
1317→                v.performClick()
1318→                return@setOnTouchListener gestureDetector!!.onTouchEvent(event)
1319→            }
1320→            whiteboard!!.handleTouchEvent(event)
1321→        }
1322→    }

1363→    @KotlinCleanup("mCurrentCard handling")
1364→    private fun suspendNoteAvailable(): Boolean {

1366→            false
1367→        } else {
1368→            getColUnsafe.db.queryScalar(
1369→                "select 1 from cards where nid = ? and id != ? and queue != " + Consts.QUEUE_TYPE_SUSPENDED + " limit 1",
1370→                currentCard!!.nid,
1371→                currentCard!!.id
1372→            ) == 1
1373→        }
1374→        // whether there exists a sibling not buried.
1375→    }

1381→        } else {
1382→            getColUnsafe.db.queryScalar(

1384→                currentCard!!.nid,
1385→                currentCard!!.id
1386→            ) == 1
1387→        }
1388→        // Whether there exists a sibling which is neither suspended nor buried

1466→        menuItem: MenuItem,

1481→                    inflate(subMenuRes)
1482→                    setOnMenuItemClickListener(onMenuItemSelection)
1483→                    show()
1484→                }
1485→            } else {

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.reviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 20→import android.view.Menu
 21→import android.view.MenuItem
 22→import androidx.annotation.IdRes
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 24→import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
 25→import com.ichi2.utils.HashUtil.hashMapInit
 26→
 27→// loads of unboxing issues, which are safe
 28→class ActionButtonStatus(private val reviewerUi: ReviewerUi) {
 29→    /**
 30→     * Custom button allocation
 31→     */
 32→    private val mCustomButtons: MutableMap<Int, Int> = hashMapInit(25) // setup's size
 33→
 34→    fun setup(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
 35→        // NOTE: the default values below should be in sync with preferences_custom_buttons.xml and reviewer.xml
 36→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_undo, "customButtonUndo", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 37→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_schedule, "customButtonScheduleCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 38→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_flag, "customButtonFlag", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 39→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_tag, "customButtonTags", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 40→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_edit, "customButtonEditCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 41→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_add_note_reviewer, "customButtonAddCard", MENU_DISABLED)
 42→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_replay, "customButtonReplay", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 43→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_card_info, "customButtonCardInfo", MENU_DISABLED)
 44→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_clear_whiteboard, "customButtonClearWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 45→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_hide_whiteboard, "customButtonShowHideWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS)
 46→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_select_tts, "customButtonSelectTts", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 47→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_open_deck_options, "customButtonDeckOptions", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 48→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_bury, "customButtonBury", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 49→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_suspend, "customButtonSuspend", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 50→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_mark_card, "customButtonMarkCard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 51→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_delete, "customButtonDelete", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 52→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_mic_tool_bar, "customButtonToggleMicToolBar", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 53→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_whiteboard, "customButtonEnableWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 54→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_toggle_stylus, "customButtonToggleStylus", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 55→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_save_whiteboard, "customButtonSaveWhiteboard", SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER)
 56→        setupButton(preferences, R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color, "customButtonWhiteboardPenColor", SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM)
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    private fun setupButton(preferences: SharedPreferences, @IdRes resourceId: Int, preferenceName: String, showAsActionType: Int) {
 60→        mCustomButtons[resourceId] = preferences.getString(
 61→            preferenceName,
 62→            showAsActionType.toString()
 63→        )!!.toInt()
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    fun setCustomButtons(menu: Menu) {
 67→        for ((itemId, value) in mCustomButtons) {
 68→            if (value != MENU_DISABLED) {
 69→                val item = menu.findItem(itemId)
 70→                item.setShowAsAction(value)
 71→                val icon = item.icon
 72→                item.isEnabled = !reviewerUi.isControlBlocked
 73→                if (icon != null) {
 74→                    /* Ideally, we want to give feedback to users that
 75→                    buttons are disabled.  However, some actions are
 76→                    expected to be so quick that the visual feedback
 77→                    is useless and is only seen as a flickering.
 78→
 79→                    We use a heuristic to decide whether the next card
 80→                    will appear quickly or slowly.  We change the
 81→                    color only if the buttons are blocked and we
 82→                    expect the next card to take time to arrive.
 83→                    */
 84→                    val mutableIcon = icon.mutate()
 85→                    if (reviewerUi.controlBlocked == ReviewerUi.ControlBlock.SLOW) {
 86→                        mutableIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 87→                    } else {
 88→                        mutableIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 89→                    }
 90→                }
 91→            } else {
 92→                menu.findItem(itemId).isVisible = false
 93→            }
 94→        }
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    fun hideWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 98→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_hide_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
 99→    }
100→
101→    fun toggleStylusIsDisabled(): Boolean {
102→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_toggle_stylus] == MENU_DISABLED
103→    }
104→
105→    fun clearWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
106→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_clear_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
107→    }
108→
109→    fun selectTtsIsDisabled(): Boolean {
110→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_select_tts] == MENU_DISABLED
111→    }
112→
113→    fun saveWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
114→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_save_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
115→    }
116→
117→    fun whiteboardPenColorIsDisabled(): Boolean {
118→        return mCustomButtons[R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color] == MENU_DISABLED
119→    }
120→
121→    companion object {
122→        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_NEVER
123→        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_IF_ROOM
124→        const val SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS = MenuItem.SHOW_AS_ACTION_ALWAYS
125→        const val MENU_DISABLED = 3
126→    }
127→}
128→

 85→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 86→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 87→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 88→            collection.remove(element)
 89→            collection.add(0, element)
 90→            true
 91→        }
 92→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 93→    }

 95→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 96→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 97→            // do not reorder the elements
 98→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
 99→                return@BiFunction false
100→            }
101→            collection.add(element)
102→            return@BiFunction true
103→        }
104→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
105→    }

107→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
108→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
109→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
110→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
111→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
112→    }

115→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
116→        bindings.remove(binding)
117→        prefs.edit {
118→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
119→        }
