 25→class Binding private constructor(val modifierKeys: ModifierKeys?, val keycode: Int?, val unicodeCharacter: Char?, val gesture: Gesture?) {
 26→    constructor(gesture: Gesture?) : this(null, null, null, gesture)
 27→
 28→    private fun getKeyCodePrefix(): String {
 29→        val keyPrefix = KEY_PREFIX.toString()
 30→
 31→        if (keycode == null) {
 32→            return keyPrefix
 33→        }
 34→
 35→        if (KeyEvent.isGamepadButton(keycode)) {
 36→            return GAMEPAD_PREFIX
 37→        }
 38→
 39→        return keyPrefix
 40→    }
 41→    fun toDisplayString(context: Context?): String {
 42→        val string = StringBuilder()
 43→        when {
 44→            keycode != null -> {
 45→                string.append(getKeyCodePrefix())
 46→                string.append(' ')
 47→                string.append(modifierKeys!!.toString())
 48→                val keyCodeString = KeyEvent.keyCodeToString(keycode)
 49→                // replace "Button" as we use the gamepad icon
 50→                string.append(StringUtil.toTitleCase(keyCodeString.replace("KEYCODE_", "").replace("BUTTON_", "").replace('_', ' ')))
 51→            }
 52→            unicodeCharacter != null -> {
 53→                string.append(KEY_PREFIX)
 54→                string.append(' ')
 55→                string.append(modifierKeys!!.toString())
 56→                string.append(unicodeCharacter)
 57→            }
 58→            gesture != null -> {
 59→                string.append(gesture.toDisplayString(context!!))
 60→            }
 61→        }
 62→        return string.toString()
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    override fun toString(): String {
 66→        val string = StringBuilder()
 67→        when {
 68→            keycode != null -> {
 69→                string.append(KEY_PREFIX)
 70→                string.append(modifierKeys!!.toString())
 71→                string.append(keycode)
 72→            }
 73→            unicodeCharacter != null -> {
 74→                string.append(UNICODE_PREFIX)
 75→                string.append(modifierKeys!!.toString())
 76→                string.append(unicodeCharacter)
 77→            }
 78→            gesture != null -> {
 79→                string.append(GESTURE_PREFIX)
 80→                string.append(gesture)
 81→            }
 82→        }
 83→        return string.toString()
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    val isValid: Boolean get() = isKey || gesture != null
 87→    val isKeyCode: Boolean get() = keycode != null
 88→
 89→    val isKey: Boolean
 90→        get() = isKeyCode || unicodeCharacter != null
 91→
 92→    val isGesture: Boolean = gesture != null

 94→    open class ModifierKeys internal constructor(private val shift: Boolean, private val ctrl: Boolean, private val alt: Boolean) {
 95→        fun matches(event: KeyEvent): Boolean {
 96→            // return false if Ctrl+1 is pressed and 1 is expected
 97→            return shiftMatches(event) && ctrlMatches(event) && altMatches(event)
 98→        }
 99→
100→        private fun shiftMatches(event: KeyEvent): Boolean = shift == event.isShiftPressed
101→
102→        private fun ctrlMatches(event: KeyEvent): Boolean = ctrl == event.isCtrlPressed
103→
104→        private fun altMatches(event: KeyEvent): Boolean = altMatches(event.isAltPressed)
105→
106→        open fun shiftMatches(shiftPressed: Boolean): Boolean = shift == shiftPressed
107→
108→        fun ctrlMatches(ctrlPressed: Boolean): Boolean = ctrl == ctrlPressed
109→
110→        fun altMatches(altPressed: Boolean): Boolean = alt == altPressed
111→
112→        override fun toString(): String {
113→            val string = StringBuilder()
114→            if (ctrl) {
115→                string.append("Ctrl+")
116→            }
117→            if (alt) {
118→                string.append("Alt+")
119→            }
120→            if (shift) {
121→                string.append("Shift+")
122→            }
123→            return string.toString()
124→        }
125→
126→        companion object {
127→            fun none(): ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys(shift = false, ctrl = false, alt = false)
128→
129→            fun ctrl(): ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys(shift = false, ctrl = true, alt = false)
130→
131→            fun shift(): ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys(shift = true, ctrl = false, alt = false)
132→
133→            fun alt(): ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys(shift = false, ctrl = false, alt = true)
134→
135→            /**
136→             * Parses a [ModifierKeys] from a string.
137→             * @param s The string to parse
138→             * @return The [ModifierKeys], and the remainder of the string
139→             */
140→            fun parse(s: String): Pair<ModifierKeys, String> {
141→                var modifiers = none()
142→                val plus = s.lastIndexOf("+")
143→                if (plus == -1) {
144→                    return Pair(modifiers, s)
145→                }
146→                modifiers = fromString(s.substring(0, plus + 1))
147→                return Pair(modifiers, s.substring(plus + 1))
148→            }
149→
150→            fun fromString(from: String): ModifierKeys =
151→                ModifierKeys(from.contains("Shift"), from.contains("Ctrl"), from.contains("Alt"))
152→        }
153→    }
154→
155→    /** Modifier keys which cannot be defined by a binding  */
156→    private class AppDefinedModifierKeys private constructor() : ModifierKeys(false, false, false) {
157→        override fun shiftMatches(shiftPressed: Boolean): Boolean = true
158→
159→        companion object {
160→            /**
161→             * Specifies a keycode combination binding from an unknown input device
162→             * Should be due to the "default" key bindings and never from user input
163→             *
164→             * If we do not know what the device is, "*" could be a key on the keyboard or Shift + 8
165→             *
166→             * So we need to ignore shift, rather than match it to a value
167→             *
168→             * If we have bindings in the app, then we know whether we need shift or not (in actual fact, we should
169→             * be fine to use keycodes).
170→             */
171→            fun allowShift(): ModifierKeys = AppDefinedModifierKeys()
172→        }
173→    }
174→
175→    companion object {
176→        const val FORBIDDEN_UNICODE_CHAR = MappableBinding.PREF_SEPARATOR
177→
178→        /**
179→         * https://www.fileformat.info/info/unicode/char/2328/index.htm (Keyboard)
180→         * This is not usable on API 21 or 22
181→         */
182→        const val KEY_PREFIX = '\u2328'
183→
184→        /** https://www.fileformat.info/info/unicode/char/235d/index.htm (similar to a finger)  */
185→        const val GESTURE_PREFIX = '\u235D'
186→
187→        /** https://www.fileformat.info/info/unicode/char/2705/index.htm - checkmark (often used in URLs for unicode)
188→         * Only used for serialisation. [.KEY_PREFIX] is used for display.
189→         */
190→        const val UNICODE_PREFIX = '\u2705'
191→
192→        const val GAMEPAD_PREFIX = "🎮"
193→
194→        /** This returns multiple bindings due to the "default" implementation not knowing what the keycode for a button is  */
195→        fun key(event: KeyEvent): List<Binding> {
196→            val modifiers = ModifierKeys(event.isShiftPressed, event.isCtrlPressed, event.isAltPressed)
197→            val ret: MutableList<Binding> = ArrayList()
198→            val keyCode = event.keyCode
199→            if (keyCode != 0) {
200→                ret.add(keyCode(modifiers, keyCode))
201→            }
202→
203→            // passing in metaState: 0 means that Ctrl+1 returns '1' instead of '\0'
204→            // NOTE: We do not differentiate on upper/lower case via KeyEvent.META_CAPS_LOCK_ON
205→            val unicodeChar = event.getUnicodeChar(event.metaState and (KeyEvent.META_SHIFT_ON or KeyEvent.META_NUM_LOCK_ON))
206→            if (unicodeChar != 0) {
207→                try {
208→                    ret.add(unicode(modifiers, unicodeChar.toChar()))
209→                } catch (e: Exception) {
210→                    Timber.w(e)
211→                }
212→            }
213→
214→            return ret
215→        }
216→
217→        /**
218→         * Specifies a unicode binding from an unknown input device
219→         * See [AppDefinedModifierKeys]
220→         */
221→        fun unicode(unicodeChar: Char): Binding =
222→            unicode(AppDefinedModifierKeys.allowShift(), unicodeChar)
223→
224→        fun unicode(modifierKeys: ModifierKeys?, unicodeChar: Char): Binding {
225→            if (unicodeChar == FORBIDDEN_UNICODE_CHAR) return unknown()
226→            return Binding(modifierKeys, null, unicodeChar, null)
227→        }
228→
229→        fun keyCode(keyCode: Int): Binding = keyCode(ModifierKeys.none(), keyCode)
230→
231→        fun keyCode(modifiers: ModifierKeys?, keyCode: Int): Binding =
232→            Binding(modifiers, keyCode, null, null)
233→
234→        fun gesture(gesture: Gesture?): Binding = Binding(null, null, null, gesture)
235→
236→        @VisibleForTesting
237→        fun unknown(): Binding = Binding(ModifierKeys.none(), null, null, null)
238→
239→        fun fromString(from: String): Binding {
240→            if (from.isEmpty()) return unknown()
241→            try {
242→                return when (from[0]) {
243→                    GESTURE_PREFIX -> {
244→                        gesture(Gesture.valueOf(from.substring(1)))
245→                    }
246→                    UNICODE_PREFIX -> {
247→                        val parsed = ModifierKeys.parse(from.substring(1))
248→                        unicode(parsed.first, parsed.second[0])
249→                    }
250→                    KEY_PREFIX -> {
251→                        val parsed = ModifierKeys.parse(from.substring(1))
252→                        val keyCode = parsed.second.toInt()
253→                        keyCode(parsed.first, keyCode)
254→                    }
255→                    else -> unknown()
256→                }
257→            } catch (ex: Exception) {
258→                Timber.w(ex)
259→            }
260→            return unknown()
261→        }
262→    }
263→}

 64→                .setBatchingEnabled(true)

 66→                .setBatchSize(1) // until this handles application termination we will lose hits if batch>1
 67→            sAnalytics = GoogleAnalytics.builder()
 68→                .withTrackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 69→                .withConfig(gaConfig)
 70→                .withDefaultRequest(

 73→                        .applicationName(context.getString(R.string.app_name))
 74→                        .applicationVersion(BuildConfig.VERSION_CODE.toString())
 75→                        .applicationId(BuildConfig.APPLICATION_ID)

 80→                .withHttpClient(OkHttpClientImpl(gaConfig))

 84→        val userPrefs = context.sharedPrefs()

 86→        userPrefs.registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener { sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String? ->
 87→            if (key == ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY) {

 89→                Timber.i("Setting analytics opt-in to: %b", newValue)

111→        Timber.d("setDevMode() re-configuring for development analytics tagging")
112→        sAnalyticsTrackingId = "UA-125800786-2"
113→        sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = 100
114→        reInitialize()
115→    }
116→
117→    /**
118→     * We want to send an analytics hit on any exception, then chain to other handlers (e.g., ACRA)
119→     */
120→    @Synchronized
121→    private fun installDefaultExceptionHandler() {
122→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = Thread.getDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler()
123→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler { thread: Thread?, throwable: Throwable ->
124→            sendAnalyticsException(throwable, true)
125→            if (thread == null) {
126→                Timber.w("unexpected: thread was null")
127→                return@setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler
128→            }
129→            sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler!!.uncaughtException(thread, throwable)
130→        }
131→    }
132→
133→    /**
134→     * Reset the default exception handler
135→     */
136→    @Synchronized
137→    private fun unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler() {
138→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler)
139→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = null
140→    }
141→
142→    /**
143→     * Allow users to enable or disable analytics
144→     */
145→    @set:Synchronized
146→    private var optIn: Boolean
147→        get() {
148→            Timber.d("getOptIn() status: %s", sOptIn)
149→            return sOptIn
150→        }
151→        private set(optIn) {
152→            Timber.i("setOptIn(): from %s to %s", sOptIn, optIn)
153→            sOptIn = optIn
154→            sAnalytics!!.flush()
155→            sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled = optIn
156→            sAnalytics!!.performSamplingElection()
157→            Timber.d("setOptIn() optIn / sAnalytics.config().enabled(): %s/%s", sOptIn, sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled)
158→        }

160→    /**
161→     * Set the analytics up to log things, goes to hit validator. Experimental.
162→     *
163→     * @param dryRun set to true if you want to log analytics hit but not dispatch
164→     */
165→    @Synchronized
166→    fun setDryRun(dryRun: Boolean) {
167→        Timber.i("setDryRun(): %s, warning dryRun is experimental", dryRun)
168→    }
169→
170→    /**
171→     * Re-Initialize the analytics provider
172→     */
173→    @Synchronized
174→    fun reInitialize() {
175→        // send any pending async hits, re-chain default exception handlers and re-init
176→        Timber.i("reInitialize()")
177→        sAnalytics!!.flush()
178→        sAnalytics = null
179→        unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler()
180→        initialize(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext)
181→    }
182→
183→    /**
184→     * Submit a screen for aggregation / analysis.
185→     * Intended for use to determine if / how features are being used
186→     *
187→     * @param object the result of Object.getClass().getSimpleName() will be used as the screen tag
188→     */
189→    @KotlinCleanup("rename object")
190→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`: Any) {
191→        sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`.javaClass.simpleName)
192→    }
193→
194→    /**
195→     * Submit a screen display with a synthetic name for aggregation / analysis
196→     * Intended for use if your class handles multiple screens you want to track separately
197→     *
198→     * @param screenName screenName the name to show in analysis reports
199→     */
200→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(screenName: String) {
201→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsScreenView(): %s", screenName)
202→        if (!optIn) {
203→            return
204→        }
205→        sAnalytics!!.screenView().screenName(screenName).sendAsync()
206→    }
207→
208→    /**
209→     * Send a detailed arbitrary analytics event, with noun/verb pairs and extra data if needed
210→     *
211→     * @param category the category of event, make your own but use a constant so reporting is good
212→     * @param action   the action the user performed
213→     * @param value    A value for the event, Integer.MIN_VALUE signifies caller shouldn't send the value
214→     * @param label    A label for the event, may be null
215→     */
216→    fun sendAnalyticsEvent(category: String, action: String, value: Int? = null, label: String? = null) {
217→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsEvent() category/action/value/label: %s/%s/%s/%s", category, action, value, label)
218→        if (!optIn) {
219→            return
220→        }
221→        val event = sAnalytics!!.event().eventCategory(category).eventAction(action)
222→        if (label != null) {
223→            event.eventLabel(label)
224→        }
225→        if (value != null) {
226→            event.eventValue(value)
227→        }
228→        event.sendAsync()
229→    }
230→
231→    /**
232→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis, parsed from the exception information
233→     *
234→     * @param t     Throwable to send for analysis
235→     * @param fatal whether it was fatal or not
236→     */
237→    fun sendAnalyticsException(t: Throwable, fatal: Boolean) {
238→        sendAnalyticsException(getCause(t).toString(), fatal)
239→    }
240→
241→    @KotlinCleanup("convert to sequence")
242→    fun getCause(t: Throwable): Throwable {
243→        var cause: Throwable?
244→        var result = t
245→        while (null != result.cause.also { cause = it } && result != cause) {
246→            result = cause!!
247→        }
248→        return result
249→    }
250→
251→    /**
252→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis
253→     *
254→     * @param description API limited to 100 characters, truncated here to 100 if needed
255→     * @param fatal       whether it was fatal or not
256→     */
257→    fun sendAnalyticsException(description: String, fatal: Boolean) {
258→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsException() description/fatal: %s/%s", description, fatal)
259→        if (!sOptIn) {
260→            return
261→        }
262→        sAnalytics!!.exception().exceptionDescription(description).exceptionFatal(fatal).sendAsync()
263→    }
264→
265→    internal fun canGetDefaultUserAgent(): Boolean {
266→        // #5502 - getDefaultUserAgent starts a WebView. We can't have two WebViews with the same data directory.
267→        // But ACRA starts an :acra process which does not terminate when AnkiDroid is restarted. https://crbug.com/558377
268→
269→        // if we're not under the ACRA process then we're fine to initialize a WebView
270→        return if (!ACRA.isACRASenderServiceProcess()) {
271→            true
272→        } else {
273→            hasSetDataDirectory()
274→        }
275→
276→        // If we have a custom data directory, then the crash will not occur.
277→    }
278→
279→    // A listener on this preference handles the rest
280→    var isEnabled: Boolean
281→        get() {
282→            val userPrefs = AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs()
283→            return userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
284→        }
285→        set(value) {
286→            // A listener on this preference handles the rest
287→            AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs().edit {
288→                putBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, value)
289→            }
290→        }

293→    fun resetForTests() {
294→        sAnalytics = null
295→    }

297→    /**
298→     * An Android-specific device config generator. Without this it's "Desktop" and unknown for all hardware.
299→     * It is interesting to us what devices people use though (for instance: is Amazon Kindle support worth it?
300→     * Is anyone still using e-ink devices? How many people are on tablets? ChromeOS?)
301→     */
302→    private class AndroidDefaultRequest : DefaultRequest() {
303→        fun setAndroidRequestParameters(context: Context): DefaultRequest {
304→            // Are we running on really large screens or small screens? Send raw screen size
305→            try {
306→                val size = DisplayUtils.getDisplayDimensions(context)
307→                this.screenResolution(size.x.toString() + "x" + size.y)
308→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
309→                Timber.w(e)
310→                // nothing much to do here, it means we couldn't get WindowManager
311→            }
312→
313→            // We can have up to 20 of these - there might be other things we want to know
314→            // but simply seeing what hardware we are running on should be useful
315→            this.customDimension(1, Build.VERSION.RELEASE) // systemVersion, e.g. "7.1.1"  for Android 7.1.1
316→            this.customDimension(2, Build.BRAND) // brand e.g. "OnePlus"
317→            this.customDimension(3, Build.MODEL) // model e.g. "ONEPLUS A6013" for the 6T
318→            this.customDimension(4, Build.BOARD) // deviceId e.g. "sdm845" for the 6T
319→
320→            // This is important for google to auto-fingerprint us for default reporting
321→            // It is not possible to set operating system explicitly, there is no API or analytics parameter for it
322→            // Instead they respond that they auto-parse User-Agent strings for analytics attribution
323→            // For maximum analytics built-in report compatibility we will send the official WebView User-Agent string
324→            try {
325→                if (canGetDefaultUserAgent()) {
326→                    this.userAgent(WebSettings.getDefaultUserAgent(context))
327→                } else {
328→                    this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
329→                }
330→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
331→                Timber.w(e)
332→                // Catch RuntimeException as WebView initialization blows up in unpredictable ways
333→                // but analytics should never be a show-stopper
334→                this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
335→            }
336→            return this
337→        }
338→    }

 17→/**
 18→ * With the Rust backend, operations that modify the collection return a description of changes (OpChanges).
 19→ * The UI can subscribe to these changes, so it can update itself when actions have been performed
 20→ * (eg, the deck list can check if studyQueues has been updated, and if so, it will redraw the list).
 21→ *
 22→ * The optional handler argument can be used so that the initiator of an action can tell when a
 23→ * OpChanges action was caused by itself. This can be useful when the default change behaviour
 24→ * should be ignored, in favour of specific handling (eg the UI wishes to directly update the
 25→ * displayed flag, without redrawing the entire review screen).
 26→ */
 27→
 28→package com.ichi2.libanki
 29→
 30→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 31→import anki.collection.OpChanges
 32→import anki.collection.OpChangesAfterUndo
 33→import anki.collection.OpChangesWithCount
 34→import anki.collection.OpChangesWithId
 35→import anki.import_export.ImportResponse
 36→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.withCol
 37→import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
 38→import kotlinx.coroutines.withContext
 39→import java.lang.ref.WeakReference
 40→
 41→object ChangeManager {
 42→    interface Subscriber {
 43→        /**
 44→         * Called after a backend method invoked via col.op() or col.opWithProgress()
 45→         * has modified the collection. Subscriber should inspect the changes, and update
 46→         * the UI if necessary.
 47→         */
 48→        fun opExecuted(changes: OpChanges, handler: Any?)
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    private val subscribers = mutableListOf<WeakReference<Subscriber>>()
 52→
 53→    fun subscribe(subscriber: Subscriber) {
 54→        subscribers.add(WeakReference(subscriber))
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    private fun notifySubscribers(changes: OpChanges, handler: Any?) {
 58→        val expired = mutableListOf<WeakReference<Subscriber>>()
 59→        for (subscriber in subscribers) {
 60→            val ref = subscriber.get()
 61→            if (ref == null) {
 62→                expired.add(subscriber)
 63→            } else {
 64→                ref.opExecuted(changes, handler)
 65→            }
 66→        }
 67→        for (item in expired) {
 68→            subscribers.remove(item)
 69→        }
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    @VisibleForTesting
 73→    fun clearSubscribers() {
 74→        subscribers.clear()
 75→    }
 76→
 77→    internal fun <T> notifySubscribers(changes: T, initiator: Any?) {
 78→        val opChanges = when (changes) {
 79→            is OpChanges -> changes
 80→            is OpChangesWithCount -> changes.changes
 81→            is OpChangesWithId -> changes.changes
 82→            is OpChangesAfterUndo -> changes.changes
 83→            is ImportResponse -> changes.changes
 84→            else -> TODO("unhandled change type")
 85→        }
 86→        notifySubscribers(opChanges, initiator)
 87→    }
 88→}
 89→
 90→/** Wrap a routine that returns OpChanges* or similar undo info with this
 91→ * to notify change subscribers of the changes. */
 92→suspend fun <T> undoableOp(handler: Any? = null, block: Collection.() -> T): T {
 93→    return withCol {
 94→        block()
 95→    }.also {
 96→        withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
 97→            ChangeManager.notifySubscribers(it, handler)
 98→        }
 99→    }
100→}

13→package com.ichi2.ui
14→
15→import android.app.Activity
16→import android.content.Context
17→import android.content.ContextWrapper
18→import android.view.MenuItem
19→import android.view.View
20→import android.widget.ImageButton
21→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
22→import androidx.appcompat.widget.TooltipCompat
23→import com.ichi2.anki.ActionProviderCompat
24→import com.ichi2.anki.R
25→
26→/**
27→ * An Rtl version of a normal action view, where the drawable is mirrored
28→ */
29→class RtlCompliantActionProvider(context: Context) : ActionProviderCompat(context) {
30→    @VisibleForTesting
31→    val mActivity: Activity
32→
33→    /**
34→     * The action to perform when clicking the associated menu item. By default this delegates to
35→     * the activity's [Activity.onOptionsItemSelected] callback, but a custom action can be set and
36→     * used instead.
37→     */
38→    var clickHandler: (Activity, MenuItem) -> Unit = { activity, menuItem ->
39→        activity.onOptionsItemSelected(menuItem)
40→    }
41→
42→    override fun onCreateActionView(forItem: MenuItem): View {
43→        val actionView = ImageButton(context, null, android.R.attr.actionButtonStyle)
44→        TooltipCompat.setTooltipText(actionView, forItem.title)
45→        forItem.icon?.let {
46→            it.isAutoMirrored = true
47→            actionView.setImageDrawable(it)
48→        }
49→        actionView.id = R.id.action_undo
50→        actionView.setOnClickListener {
51→            if (!forItem.isEnabled) {
52→                return@setOnClickListener
53→            }
54→            clickHandler(mActivity, forItem)
55→        }
56→        return actionView
57→    }
58→
59→    companion object {
60→        /**
61→         * Unwrap a context to get the base activity back.
62→         * @param context a context that may be of type [ContextWrapper]
63→         * @return The activity of the passed context
64→         */
65→        private fun unwrapContext(context: Context): Activity {
66→            var unwrappedContext: Context? = context
67→            while (unwrappedContext !is Activity && unwrappedContext is ContextWrapper) {
68→                unwrappedContext = unwrappedContext.baseContext
69→            }
70→            return if (unwrappedContext is Activity) {
71→                unwrappedContext
72→            } else {
73→                throw ClassCastException("Passed context should be either an instanceof Activity or a ContextWrapper wrapping an Activity")
74→            }
75→        }
76→    }
77→
78→    init {
79→        mActivity = unwrapContext(context)
80→    }
81→}

 18→package com.ichi2.anki
 19→
 20→import android.content.Context
 21→import android.view.View
 22→import android.widget.FrameLayout
 23→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 24→import androidx.recyclerview.widget.LinearLayoutManager
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.OnboardingUtils.Companion.addFeatures
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.OnboardingUtils.Companion.isVisited
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.OnboardingUtils.Companion.setVisited
 28→import com.ichi2.utils.HandlerUtils.executeFunctionUsingHandler
 29→
 30→/**
 31→ * Suppose a tutorial needs to be added for an activity called MyActivity.
 32→ * Steps for doing it:
 33→ *
 34→ * 1. If an inner class for MyActivity exists, then use it otherwise create a new class
 35→ * inside Onboarding and initialise an object of that class inside MyActivity. In this case,
 36→ * call onCreate() if the tutorial needs to be displayed when the screen is opened and does not have
 37→ * any particular time or view visibility on which it depends. If the class already exists,
 38→ * go to step 3.
 39→ *
 40→ * 2. If MyActivity does not already exist, then create it by extending Onboarding and
 41→ * then create a new enum class implementing OnboardingFlag.
 42→ *
 43→ * 3. Create a new method to display the tutorial.
 44→ *
 45→ * 4. Add the function using TutorialArgument data class to the list of tutorials in the init block
 46→ * if it has to be invoked which the screen is opened. If the function has to be invoked at a particular
 47→ * time, then call it from MyActivity.
 48→ *
 49→ * 5. For any extra condition that needs to be checked before displaying a tutorial, add it as the
 50→ * 'mOnboardingCondition' parameter in TutorialArguments.
 51→ */
 52→
 53→// Contains classes for various screens. Each sub-class has methods to show onboarding tutorials.
 54→// TODO: Move OnboardingUtils.featureConstants to a DI container rather than a mutable singleton
 55→abstract class Onboarding<Feature>(
 56→    private val context: Context,
 57→    val tutorials: MutableList<TutorialArguments<Feature>>
 58→) where Feature : Enum<Feature>, Feature : OnboardingFlag {
 59→
 60→    companion object {
 61→        // Constants being used for onboarding preferences should not be modified.
 62→        const val DECK_PICKER_ONBOARDING = "DeckPickerOnboarding"
 63→        const val REVIEWER_ONBOARDING = "ReviewerOnboarding"
 64→        const val NOTE_EDITOR_ONBOARDING = "NoteEditorOnboarding"
 65→        const val CARD_BROWSER_ONBOARDING = "CardBrowserOnboarding"
 66→
 67→        init {
 68→            addDefaultFeatures()
 69→        }
 70→
 71→        @VisibleForTesting
 72→        fun resetOnboardingForTesting() {
 73→            OnboardingUtils.featureConstants.clear()
 74→            addDefaultFeatures()
 75→        }
 76→
 77→        private fun addDefaultFeatures() {
 78→            addFeatures(
 79→                listOf(
 80→                    DECK_PICKER_ONBOARDING,
 81→                    REVIEWER_ONBOARDING,
 82→                    NOTE_EDITOR_ONBOARDING,
 83→                    CARD_BROWSER_ONBOARDING
 84→                )
 85→            )
 86→        }
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    /**
 90→     * Contains the logic for iterating through various tutorials of a screen and displaying the first one
 91→     * in the list which is not visited yet and condition (if any) also holds true for it.
 92→     *
 93→     */
 94→    fun onCreate() {
 95→        tutorials.forEach {
 96→            // If tutorial is visited or condition is false then return from the loop.
 97→            if (isVisited(it.featureIdentifier, context) || it.onboardingCondition?.invoke() == false) {
 98→                return@forEach
 99→            }
100→
101→            // Invoke the function to display the tutorial.
102→            it.onboardingFunction.invoke()
103→
104→            // Return so that other tutorials are not displayed.
105→            return
106→        }
107→    }
108→
109→    /**
110→     * Arguments required to handle the tutorial for a screen.
111→     *
112→     * @param featureIdentifier Enum constant for the feature.
113→     * @param onboardingFunction Function which has to be invoked if the tutorial needs to be displayed.
114→     * @param onboardingCondition Condition to be checked before displaying the tutorial. Tutorial should
115→     * be displayed only if mOnboardingCondition is not null and returns true. Default value is null
116→     * which indicates that no condition is required.
117→     */
118→    data class TutorialArguments<Feature>(
119→        val featureIdentifier: Feature,
120→        val onboardingFunction: () -> Unit,
121→        val onboardingCondition: (() -> Boolean)? = null
122→    )
123→            where Feature : Enum<Feature>, Feature : OnboardingFlag

125→    class DeckPicker(
126→        private val activityContext: com.ichi2.anki.DeckPicker,
127→        private val recyclerViewLayoutManager: LinearLayoutManager
128→    ) : Onboarding<DeckPicker.DeckPickerOnboardingEnum>(activityContext, mutableListOf()) {
129→
130→        init {
131→            tutorials.add(TutorialArguments(DeckPickerOnboardingEnum.FAB, this::showTutorialForFABIfNew))
132→            tutorials.add(TutorialArguments(DeckPickerOnboardingEnum.DECK_NAME, this::showTutorialForDeckIfNew, activityContext::hasAtLeastOneDeckBeingDisplayed))
133→            tutorials.add(TutorialArguments(DeckPickerOnboardingEnum.COUNTS_LAYOUT, this::showTutorialForCountsLayoutIfNew, activityContext::hasAtLeastOneDeckBeingDisplayed))
134→        }
135→
136→        private fun showTutorialForFABIfNew() {
137→            CustomMaterialTapTargetPromptBuilder(activityContext, DeckPickerOnboardingEnum.FAB)
138→                .createCircleWithDimmedBackground()
139→                .setFocalColourResource(R.color.material_blue_500)
140→                .setDismissedListener { onCreate() }
141→                .setTarget(R.id.fab_main)
142→                .setPrimaryText(R.string.fab_tutorial_title)
143→                .setSecondaryText(R.string.fab_tutorial_desc)
144→                .show()
145→        }
146→
147→        private fun showTutorialForDeckIfNew() {
148→            CustomMaterialTapTargetPromptBuilder(activityContext, DeckPickerOnboardingEnum.DECK_NAME)
149→                .createRectangleWithDimmedBackground()
150→                .setDismissedListener { showTutorialForCountsLayoutIfNew() }
151→                .setTarget(recyclerViewLayoutManager.getChildAt(0)?.findViewById(R.id.deck_name_linear_layout))
152→                .setPrimaryText(R.string.start_studying)
153→                .setSecondaryText(R.string.start_studying_desc)
154→                .show()
155→        }
156→
157→        private fun showTutorialForCountsLayoutIfNew() {
158→            CustomMaterialTapTargetPromptBuilder(activityContext, DeckPickerOnboardingEnum.COUNTS_LAYOUT)
159→                .createRectangleWithDimmedBackground()
160→                .setTarget(recyclerViewLayoutManager.getChildAt(0)?.findViewById(R.id.counts_layout))
161→                .setPrimaryText(R.string.menu__study_options)
162→                .setSecondaryText(R.string.study_options_desc)
163→                .show()
164→        }
165→
166→        enum class DeckPickerOnboardingEnum(var value: Int) : OnboardingFlag {
167→            FAB(0), DECK_NAME(1), COUNTS_LAYOUT(2);
168→
169→            override fun getOnboardingEnumValue(): Int {
170→                return value
171→            }
172→
173→            override fun getFeatureConstant(): String {
174→                return DECK_PICKER_ONBOARDING
175→            }
176→        }
177→    }

188→            CustomMaterialTapTargetPromptBuilder(activityContext, ReviewerOnboardingEnum.SHOW_ANSWER)
189→                .createRectangleWithDimmedBackground()

194→                .show()

197→        /**
198→         * Called when the difficulty buttons are displayed after clicking on 'Show Answer'.
199→         */

201→            if (isVisited(ReviewerOnboardingEnum.DIFFICULTY_RATING, activityContext)) {

203→            }
204→
205→            CustomMaterialTapTargetPromptBuilder(activityContext, ReviewerOnboardingEnum.DIFFICULTY_RATING)
206→                .createRectangleWithDimmedBackground()

213→        private fun showTutorialForFlagIfNew() {
214→            // Handler is required here to show feature prompt on menu items. Reference: https://github.com/sjwall/MaterialTapTargetPrompt/issues/73#issuecomment-320681655
215→            executeFunctionUsingHandler {
216→                CustomMaterialTapTargetPromptBuilder(activityContext, ReviewerOnboardingEnum.FLAG)
217→                    .createCircle()
218→                    .setFocalColourResource(R.color.material_blue_500)
219→                    .setTarget(R.id.action_flag)
220→                    .setPrimaryText(R.string.menu_flag_card)
221→                    .setSecondaryText(R.string.flag_card_desc)
222→                    .show()
223→            }
224→        }

229→        fun onUndoButtonEnabled() {
230→            if (isVisited(ReviewerOnboardingEnum.UNDO, activityContext)) {
231→                return
232→            }
233→
234→            CustomMaterialTapTargetPromptBuilder(activityContext, ReviewerOnboardingEnum.UNDO)
235→                .createCircleWithDimmedBackground()
236→                .setFocalColourResource(R.color.material_blue_500)
237→                .setTarget(R.id.action_undo)
238→                .setPrimaryText(R.string.undo)
239→                .setSecondaryText(R.string.undo_desc)
240→                .show()
241→        }

245→

248→            }
249→
